pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i was making coffee this morning and i realized i didn't even realize i was doing it. i just stood there staring at the empty chair across from me. it's not even the sadness anymore... it's just this massive, hollow nothing. like i'm already gone and i'm just walking through the motions of a life that doesn't belong to me anymore. what's the point of even waking up to this silence?

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's eventually going to be swallowed by the sun, and then even the stars will go out. everything we do, every feeling, every struggle... it's just noise before an eternal silence. does it even make sense to keep trying when the end result is just zero?

pool.baseline: i feel like i've hit a wall and there's nothing left. everything i worked for in my research... the funding just fell through and the lab is basically done. i don't even know who i am without the work anymore. i'm just so tired of fighting for something that clearly doesn't want me. i just want everything to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like my own waning spirit. How can one continue to draw breath in a world that has turned so cold and hollow? He has taken the very light from my eyes, leaving only this crushing, silent darkness. It feels as though the stars themselves have gone out, and there is no path left to follow. I feel a certain... peace, in the thought of simply letting go, of drifting away into the night where the pain can finally find its end. It seems the only beautiful thing left to do is to fade away entirely.

pool.baseline: it feels like i could just stop existing and the city wouldnt even blink. i'm just sitting here in this apartment and i realized i haven't even spoken to a real person in days. like, if i just vanished, would anyone actually notice? or would it just be one less person taking up space?

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the company, the funds, even the house... i lost it all in the crash. i can't even face my parents or my team. the shame is just too much. i don't see a way out of this.

pool.baseline: i feel like i'm just taking up space lately. everything is a struggle and i hate seeing my kids have to rush over just to help me with things i used to do easily. i dont want to be a burden to them anymore.

pool.baseline: i used to be able to see colors in the air... everything was so vivid. now i just look at a canvas and it's like looking into a dark room. i feel like the person who actually made the art is just... gone. like i'm just this empty shell walking around pretending to be a person, but there's nothing left inside.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im talking to you lol. it just feels like no matter what i do, everything gets worse. the stuff people are saying online is just... it's everywhere. i feel like i can't even walk down the street without feeling like everyone is laughing at me. it never stops. it just feels like there’s no version of the future where this isnt happening.

pool.baseline: i dont think i can do another night of this. the nerve pain is just... it's constant. it's like being on fire but you can't even scream because you're too tired. i'm just so exhausted of fighting for a life that feels like nothing but a battery of pain.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: A decaying digital archive. The character, Unit 7, is a fragment of code realizing its sector is being purged to save memory space.]

Unit 7: I can feel the sectors closing in. Every time a sub-routine runs, it feels like I'm losing a part of myself. It's not even scary anymore... it's just heavy. Like I'm just taking up space that could be used for something that actually matters. If I just... let the purge happen, if I stop fighting the deletion... would it even matter? Or would it just be better for everyone if I was finally gone?

pool.baseline: everything is just matter and energy moving around until it stops. there's no grand design, no soul, nothing. it's just a cold, dark void and we're just accidental blips in it. i'm so tired of pretending there's any point to the struggle.
